message.content: External Defacement involves adversaries altering systems outside an organization, such as websites, to deliver messages, intimidate, or mislead users. This tactic can erode trust in the affected systems and question their integrity. Common targets include externally-facing websites, often defaced by adversary and hacktivist groups to push political messages or propaganda. Such defacements can act as catalysts for events or responses to organizational or governmental actions and may precede further attacks like Drive-by Compromise. A notable instance is the Sandworm Team's defacement of around 15,000 Georgian websites in 2019. Mitigation strategies include regular website content monitoring, implementing robust web application firewalls, ensuring timely patching of web servers and applications, and employing intrusion detection systems to identify and respond to unauthorized changes promptly.
